title: Garlicky Zesty Greek Chilli Spanakopita
description: A juicy, monsoon-inspired Greek dish, combining the flavors of chilli, garlic, and spinach, within a crispy, iron-teppan pan-spanakopita form, following a whole30-friendly recipe.

ingredients.name: Feta Cheese (dairy free alternative: cashew cheese)

instructions: Heat the olive oil in an iron teppan pan over medium heat.
Sauté the chopped onion and minced garlic until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the chopped red chilli and sauté for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the fresh spinach and sauté until wilted, about 2-3 minutes.
In a bowl, combine the cooked spinach mixture, crumbled feta cheese (or cashew cheese), chopped parsley, salt, and black pepper.
Roll out the spanakopita dough to the size of the iron teppan pan.
Spoon the spinach and feta mixture onto one half of the dough, leaving a 1-inch border.
Fold the other half of the dough over the filling and press the edges to seal.
Use a knife to create a decorative border around the edges of the spanakopita.
Brush the top of the spanakopita with olive oil and bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
